attributes.action: A full line Government Whip should only be used to pass Manifesto policy
attributes.additional_details: Pass a bill or set a convention that MPs should only be whipped on manifesto policies as they are the only ones with a democratic mandate. To potentially whip backbenchers on possible investigations into the actions of a Prime Minister or other government officials prevents successful scrutiny and goes against the best interests of the Nation. Scrutiny votes are a National interest and go beyond party politics, so they should be seen that way.
attributes.background: In line with recent rumours that the government is going to whip their MPs on the vote on a parliamentary sleaze enquiry into the PM, we must ensure that all Whipping only occurs on manifesto policies which are the only ones that have a democratic mandate

rejection.details: This is not something which the UK Government or Parliament is directly responsible for, as this is a matter for the individual political parties.
